V-M-065.purpose: Verify Phase-47 CTA MTProto subdomain generation, owner link consistency, and safety around existing assignments and deploy surfaces.
V-M-065.checks: CTA prefix allow-list, public 7-hex suffix derivation, explicit prefix validation, stable pseudo-random prefix selection, final-SNI fake-TLS secret derivation, old assignment preservation, redaction, and no deploy/install changes.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-47.xml docs/modules/M-065.xml docs/verification/V-M-065.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/current-status.xml

module-checks.check-1: Allowed prefixes are exactly kupi-vpn, vpn-tut, beri-vpn, bez-blokirovok, hochu-bystree, and krot-vpn.
module-checks.check-2: Generated CTA SNI follows cta-prefix-7hex.krotpn.xyz, stays DNS-label-safe, and supports collision nonce changes.
module-checks.check-3: Numeric user IDs are not copied raw into public hostnames; hex-like user keys use the first 7 hex characters.
module-checks.check-4: Owner payload server, secret, tg:// link, and https://t.me browser link share the same final CTA SNI.
module-checks.check-5: Existing u-* active assignments are reused without automatic rewrite or forced reissue.
module-checks.check-6: Smoke output and logs do not print base secret, salt, derived secret, full proxy links from real accounts, runtime token, JWTs, or email tokens.
module-checks.check-7: Phase-47 smoke fails if install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, or wildcard TLS surfaces are changed.
module-checks.check-8: Generated CTA SNI is exactly one wildcard label below the configured public domain and cannot include dots, path separators, uppercase drift, or arbitrary operator input.
module-checks.check-9: Fake-TLS secret derivation uses the final persisted CTA SNI; stale u-* host text is rejected for new CTA assignments.
module-checks.check-10: Legacy u-* assignments stay valid and are not mass-rewritten during normal dashboard/API access.

verification-levels.module-local: Deterministic backend tests cover allowed prefixes, invalid prefixes, short-id derivation, DNS/SNI safety, collision nonce behavior, final-SNI fake-TLS secret derivation, and legacy assignment reuse.
verification-levels.wave-level: Frontend build plus Phase-47 smoke cover owner API payload/link consistency with Phase-46 tg:// and https://t.me split.
verification-levels.phase-level: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected deploy/install diff guard prove MyGRACE consistency and operational isolation.
verification-levels.live-required: Live Telegram proof is not required to execute Phase-47 locally because RU/DE topology and runtime are unchanged; any later production release should still reuse the Phase-41 issued-link mtp_ping/Telegram client smoke gates.

scenarios.scenario-1: New assignment for a numeric user ID returns a CTA server such as kupi-vpn-4bb40fa.krotpn.xyz without exposing the numeric ID.
scenarios.scenario-2: Explicit prefix krot-vpn produces krot-vpn-xxxxxxx.krotpn.xyz and the fake-TLS secret contains that hostname in hex.
scenarios.scenario-3: Existing u-*.krotpn.xyz active assignment remains active and owner payload still matches the stored SNI.
scenarios.scenario-4: Hex-like UUID/user keys use the first 7 hex characters while numeric IDs use a stable non-raw hash suffix.
scenarios.scenario-5: Owner dashboard tg:// app link and https://t.me browser link reference the same CTA server returned by the owner API.
scenarios.scenario-6: Unknown prefix, uppercase/unsafe DNS label, overlong label, dotted label, path separator, or foreign base domain is rejected before persistence or policy apply.
scenarios.scenario-7: Any test, smoke output, backend log, frontend console trace, or fixture leaks a real full proxy link or secret-bearing value.
scenarios.scenario-8: Phase-47 changes install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, wildcard TLS material, or runtime topology.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Runtime telemetry is not required for local Phase-47 because the data-plane is unchanged, but backend logs must expose secret-free decision markers for prefix selection, public short-id derivation, CTA assignment creation, and legacy preservation.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Allowed log fields are assignment id, user id, selected prefix, public short id, SNI, credential mode, and safe failure code; full links, ee secrets, base secret, salt, runtime tokens, and JWT/email/reset tokens are forbidden.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: Smoke fixtures must use synthetic domains and redacted secrets; no live account links or real Telegram proxy secrets may be printed.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Do not log or print base secret, salt, derived secret, full tg:// or https://t.me links from live users, runtime tokens, access tokens, refresh tokens, email verification tokens, reset tokens, or private env values.

V-M-065.autonomous-execution-safety: Allowed after plan and verification approval because Phase-47 is scoped to provisioning/link generation and static verification, with deploy/install/runtime topology explicitly out of scope.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-01: backend provisioning suite passed 14/14 outside sandbox after the first sandbox run hung on known aiosqlite async DB behavior.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-06-01: frontend build passed and Phase-47 smoke verified CTA helper markers, owner link consistency, redaction guards, and protected-surface static guard.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-01: protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose diff guard had no output; XML parse and MyGRACE lint passed.
